behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: combine commit divergence vs main, commit age threshold, and PR-open-state into one stale decision. Hypothetical elsewhere: live GitHub outages/API auth failures. MOCK-MISS: yes, failure is at module handoff (git-state + PR-state + policy). REAL-INFRA: not explicitly required as live network/OS behavior. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP agent pipeline: no. Rejection gate: System was considered, but behavior text does not require live repo/API (only correct identification logic).
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-process integration test wiring staleness evaluator with a local repo-state fixture/module plus a stub PR-state provider. Cover: (a) commits ahead + old + no open PR => stale, (b) any missing condition => not stale. Verify computed decision only.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: bootstrap/session-start path must consume stale-state and emit warning payload with branch context. Hypothetical elsewhere: terminal rendering quirks, shell startup race conditions. MOCK-MISS: yes, issue appears when startup wiring + detector + warning formatter interact. REAL-INFRA: no explicit requirement for real subprocess/OS/network. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior asks for emitted warning semantics, not real shell/process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initializer with injected stale branch state and assert one warning event/message is emitted containing actionable context (e.g., branch name and stale reason). Also assert non-stale state emits no stale warning.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: pure local mapping from computed state to fixed labels, deterministic output. Hypothetical elsewhere: upstream state computation bugs. MOCK-MISS: no, single function logic. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior explicitly scopes to a local classifier function.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test over classifier inputs to assert exact label outputs and repeatability across repeated calls.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: command is executed against live repo state and live PR state, then rendered output must reflect true classification. Hypothetical elsewhere: model-based summarization quality. MOCK-MISS: yes, mocks can hide real failures in git/PR retrieval and command wiring. REAL-INFRA: explicitly yes (live repo + live PR state). LLM-DEP: no evidence output correctness depends on model nondeterminism. MULTI-STEP agentic: no. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected; behavior text contains no AI/ML judgment step.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` end-to-end in a real test repo with real PR-state source configured; validate rendered output for two states: clean and commits-without-PR stale. Assert exact stale marker appears only in stale scenario.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: classifier must correctly consume PR lifecycle state (open/merged) and avoid stale-no-pr labeling. Hypothetical elsewhere: eventual consistency delays in remote API. MOCK-MISS: yes, bug is often at state-interpretation boundary between PR metadata and classifier. REAL-INFRA: not explicitly requiring live network. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text does not require live PR API, only correct handling of open/merged states.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with wired classifier/detector and stubbed PR-state adapter returning open and merged cases; assert result is never `stale-no-pr` for both.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: correctness must hold across real history-shape changes (rebase/squash) where hashes differ but semantic inclusion in main changes. Hypothetical elsewhere: remote provider outages. MOCK-MISS: yes, mocked commit graphs can miss git ancestry edge cases after rewrite operations. REAL-INFRA: yes, this behavior is about git history operations that are most reliably reproduced with real repo/subprocess behavior. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior explicitly targets rebase/squash history-rewrite correctness, a known real git-boundary failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: System test creating a real git repo: produce stale branch, then simulate rebase/squash merge paths that rewrite hashes; rerun detector and assert branch is not reported as unPRed stale once changes are effectively integrated.
